CORRECTS PREVIOUS FUNNEL CLOUD REPORT FROM 5 W MENDOTA. CORRECTS PREVIOUS FUNNEL CLOUD REPORT FROM 5 W MENDOTA. FUNNEL CLOUD REPORT WEST OF MENDOTA SHARED ON TWITTER. TIME AND LOCATION ARE ESTIMATED BY RADAR. RECEIVED A CALL FROM ABC30 FRESNO. THEY RECEIVED PICS OF DAMAGE AS WELL AS A PIC OF A FUNNEL CLOUD ON OXNARD ST IN MENDOTA WHICH WERE FORWARDED TO US. DAMAGE INCLUDES HEAVY ROOFING TILE, FENCING KNOCKED OVER, PATIO FURNITURE TOSSED AROUND. AFTER SITE SURVEY BY NWS HANFORD SCIENCE OFFICER, IT WAS DETERMINED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO DID TOUCH DOWN AT APPROXIMATELY 1146L. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH.

EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTH OF BOWMAN RD IN RIDGECREST AND TRACKED NORTHEAST. THE TORNADO CROSSED CHURCH AVE, THEN MOVED THROUGH A SCRAPYARD AND CROSSED INTO SAN BERNADINO COUNTY BEFORE DISSIPATING NORTHEAST OF THE STATE ROUTE 178 AND SAN BERNADINO BLVD. INTERSECTION.

STORM SURVEY TEAM TODAY DID NOT FIND ANY DAMAGE. HOWEVER AN EF0 TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND WITH 70 MPH WINDS, BASED ON RADAR SIGNATURES, VIDEO OF THE CIRCULATION FROM SOCIAL MEDIA AND NWS EMPLOYEES OBSERVATIONS. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR A MINUTE OR LESS WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 100 YARDS.

THUNDERSTORM DROPPED 1.36 INCHES OF RAIN ON COALINGA IN ONE HOUR AND FIFTEEN MINUTES. WATER COMPLETED FLOODED ALL MAJOR STREETS IN TOWN WITH SEVERAL STRUCTURES RECEIVING WATER DAMAGE. COMPARED NEARLY IDENTICAL TO 2015 FLASH FLOOD EVENT PER COALINGA FIRE.
